title: What Dermal Fillers Should You Choose？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: What Dermal Fillers Should You Choose？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So what dermal fillers should you choose so there's a huge range of dermal fillers if you're watching this in America you should have to use fda-approved products if you're watching this in much of the rest of the world it's any product that has basically been manufactured to the…

segments.text: so what dermal fillers should you choose so there's a huge range of dermal fillers if you're watching this in America you should have to use fda-approved products if you're watching this in much of the rest of the world it's any product that has basically been manufactured to the correct standard for that region in Europe we have C marking and that just tells you it's been through a validated process of manufacture it doesn't mean it's been tested on humans in the same way as FDA approved products is and this might be one reason why you choose to opt for fda-approved products because you know you've got that data on humans about how their product reacts now demo fillers where their classes medical devices do not need that interactional data that you have from FDA approved devices so that might be one way to choose which product you use the next thing is about where are you using what is the result that you're trying to get because essentially there are basically five different types of dermal filler that you might use there are some that are emulating bone they're there for structure and they're trying to give you

angles on jaw lines for example or cheeks sometimes noses they're much stiffer harder products you then have products that are more emulating fat so that's volumizing products something like giving them voluma that I use bollocks for the bone emulating structure but all manufacturers have a version of these products and you then have some that are might just be for kind of gentle contouring or volume but less about kind of big fat pads so that's an intermediate product something like Juvederm voll if I use you then have a vole Bella which is or a really thin a much thinner product which is for tear trough or surface greases so it's about getting into that derma dermal layer so that you're unfolding a crease and then finally you have skin treatments that come right on the top and there's kind of super hydrating products something like profile oh that's about making the surface as moist as possible but it isn't changing the shape or specifically unfolding deeper folds or adding volume it's all about surface treatments so those that those are essentially the five layers that you can use dermal fillers and there are many products in each of those

layers and you need it you need as a clinician to come up with a good range you're comfortable with and then get lots of practice I wouldn't suggest that you use a hundred different products you need to get to know your products really well get to know their character sticks and that will put you in good stead to give you a patient's really good advice and to predict the outcome that they are after
